narrative.headline: What 2016 Ford Explorer owners report most
narrative.summary: Structure-related complaints make up the largest share of owner reports, with steering failures and engine noise also appearing frequently across the complaint set. Owners describe a range of sudden and unexpected behaviors — including loss of steering control, unintended liftgate movement, acceleration failure, and persistent cooling fan noise. Some complaints across multiple areas note associated fires or crashes, indicating the issues can occur in consequential driving situations.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: Owners report the liftgate closing on its own while the vehicle is parked, with dealers in some cases unable to diagnose or repair the condition. · 1 fire · 4 crash · 6 injured

issues.sample: I RECENTLY RECEIVED A 2016 FORD POLICE UTILITY (EXPLORER) AS A NEW PATROL VEHICLE.  WHILE ON PATROL I SUDDENLY LOST POWER STEERING.  I RECEIVED A STEERING ASSIST FAULT MESSAGE ON THE DASH, AS WELL AS A TRACTION AND STABILITY CONTROL ERROR MESSAGE.  THE VEHICLE HAS 935 MILES ON IT
issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report sudden complete loss of power steering accompanied by a steering assist fault message on the dashboard, along with traction and stability warning indicators. · 10 crash · 7 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report loud squealing from the radiator cooling fan at idle and low speeds, with the noise recurring shortly after the fan was already replaced at a shop. · 3 fire · 3 crash · 8 injured

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle suddenly losing the ability to accelerate from a stop, requiring the engine to be shut off and restarted before movement is possible. · 4 fire · 6 crash
